adjudicator_mark.evidence: **Add a boot-time or CI self-check for RLS bypass.** A small check — either at app startup or as a CI step against a real Postgres — that runs as the configured runtime role and confirms it is neither a superuser nor `BYPASSRLS` nor the owner of any table with an RLS policy (e.g., query `pg_tables`/`pg_roles`/`has_table_privilege` or simply attempt to read cross-tenant rows and assert zero results) would catch this class of misconfiguration regardless of which specific commit or environment variable introduces it.

votes.evidence: **Add a boot-time or CI self-check for RLS bypass.** A small check — either at app startup or as a CI step ... confirms it is neither a superuser nor `BYPASSRLS` nor the owner of any table with an RLS policy

Add a boot-time or CI self-check for RLS bypass. A small check — either at app startup or as a CI step against a real Postgres — that runs as the configured runtime role and confirms it is neither a superuser nor BYPASSRLS nor the owner of any table with an RLS policy... would catch this class of misconfiguration regardless of which specific commit or environment variable introduces it.

disputes.rule: two-of-three majority: the two primary judges' marks plus the adjudicator's mark; because the primaries split one-to-one on a disputed mark, the majority equals the adjudicator's mark, and the mark stays in every denominator
